message:
feat(fp-types): add From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> impls for Scalar (br-esjjy / fd90.182)

fd90.182: ergonomic From impls for Scalar, mirroring IndexLabel's
existing From<i64>/From<&str>/From<String> at fp-index/src/lib.rs:91-105.

Before this commit users had to write:
  Scalar::Float64(3.14)
  Scalar::Int64(42)
  Scalar::Utf8("hello".to_owned())

Now they can write:
  3.14f64.into()
  42i64.into()
  "hello".into()

This is the same .into() pattern already supported on IndexLabel
(used heavily in the README's MultiIndex / case_when / loc examples).
fd90.181's integration test ran into the gap when raw f64 from a
.sum::<f64>() call wouldn't .into() a Scalar — the test had to use
explicit Scalar::Float64(total). With this commit, the same pattern
works via .into().

Variant choices:
  bool   → Bool
  i64    → Int64    (more common than Timedelta64; users wanting
                     Timedelta64 should construct it explicitly via
                     Scalar::Timedelta64(nanos) or Timedelta::parse)
  f64    → Float64
  &str   → Utf8     (via to_owned())
  String → Utf8

No type-inference ambiguity: all input types are distinct.

Verified:
- cargo test -p fp-types       — 156 passed (155 + new scalar_from_primitive_types)
- cargo test -p fp-frame --lib — 1434 passed
- cargo test -p frankenpandas  — 7 lib + 22 integration + 2 doctests pass

Pure additive API. No behavior change to existing code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
